project_name: Machine Learning Scrap Prediction for Aluminum Casting Process
problem_statement: The aluminum casting process does not currently predict whether a part will become scrap early enough to enable a timely response. This delays corrective action and can extend response time by up to 18 hours.
project_objectives: Develop a machine learning model to predict scrap risk and enable faster process-variable adjustment from the HMI.
project_timing: This project will follow a 3-month DMAIC schedule from Define in May 2026 through Control in August 2026.
in_scope: Analysis of aluminum casting process variables, machine learning model development, and operator response through HMI adjustments.
out_of_scope: Equipment replacement, full foundry process redesign, and changes outside the aluminum casting process.
project_impact_3y: This project can improve response time, support earlier correction of process conditions, and help reduce scrap in aluminum casting. Based on one operator as direct labor, the reference rate is 9.53 USD/hour (185.840919 MXN/hour), but total financial impact cannot yet be annualized because the frequency of the 18-hour saving was not provided; Finance validation is pending.
